name: General Chua's Chicken
recipeInstructions:
1. Pound the chicken slightly with a mallet to tenderize.
2. Cut into pieces measuring 3/4 by 3/4 inch.
3. Combine marinade ingredients with chicken in the order listed; set aside.
4. Mix sauce ingredients thoroughly; set aside.
5. To oil-blanch, heat wok over high heat for about 1 minute.
6. Add 2 cups of vegetable oil and heat to 350 degrees.
7. Add chicken and blanch for 3 or 4 minutes until golden brown.
8. Remove and drain the chicken, reserving the oil.
9. To stir-fry, remove all but 2 tablespoons of oil from the wok.
10. Reheat, swirling the pan to coat the sides.
11. Add chili pods and seeds; stir-fry for about 30 seconds until browned.
12. Return the chicken and add seasonings, stirring for another 30 seconds.
13. Pour in the sauce and cook until thickened.
14. Stir in sesame oil before serving.
Note: Brown the chili pods to produce aroma while using the seeds to make the dish spicy and hot. Be careful not to burn the seeds or let them get too dark.
